title: Create a new project from grid view and open it in the workbench
description: Verifies a user can search, create a new project from the hub, see it listed, and open it into the workbench.

title: Open a project from the hub into the workbench
description: Verifies that opening any existing project from the hub transitions the UI into the workbench for that project.

steps.description: Click on the first visible "Open" button on a project card
Verify element "Project workspace header" is visible

title: Switch to list mode and verify projects are displayed
description: Verifies the user can switch to list mode and see projects displayed in a tabular/list layout.

title: Filter projects using search text and confirm list updates
description: Verifies search filtering reduces the visible projects to those matching the entered text.

title: Cancel create project modal does not create a new project
description: Verifies canceling out of the create modal closes it and does not add a new project to the list.

steps.description: Verify text "Create Project" is not visible

title: Create new project requires Project ID validation before submission
description: Verifies the create modal enforces required Project ID input and prevents creation when missing.

title: View Project Info summary and project.yml preview for an opened project
description: Verifies that a user can open a project and view the Project Info tab with aggregated summary information and a project.yml preview.

steps.description: Click "Project Info" tab in the TabBar

title: Edit project description metadata and save successfully
description: Verifies that editing the Description field in Project Info can be saved and the UI reflects the updated description with a success toast.

steps.description: Click "Project Info" tab in the TabBar
Type "Updated project description for UI test" into the "Description" field

title: Saved description is reflected in Project Info summary after reopening the editor
description: Verifies that after saving a new Description, the Project Info view continues to show the updated value when revisiting metadata editing.

steps.description: Click "Project Info" tab in the TabBar
Type "Description persisted check" into the "Description" field
Verify text "Description persisted check" is visible

title: Edit tags and visibility and save successfully
description: Verifies that a user can modify project tags and visibility settings and save without errors.

steps.description: Click "Project Info" tab in the TabBar

title: Invalid visibility value shows validation error and blocks saving
description: Verifies that entering an invalid visibility value triggers a visible validation error and prevents a successful save.

steps.description: Click "Project Info" tab in the TabBar

title: Project Info shows counts/tags/cache status sections in the aggregated summary
description: Verifies that the aggregated project summary includes key sections such as counts, tags, and cache status indicators.

steps.description: Click "Project Info" tab in the TabBar

title: Open Edit metadata and cancel/close without saving does not show success toast
description: Verifies that exiting the Edit metadata UI without saving does not display a success toast.

steps.description: Click "Project Info" tab in the TabBar

title: Open a project and confirm Workbench top bar shows active project
description: Verifies that opening a project from the hub loads the workbench and displays the selected project in the top bar.

steps.description: Click on "Open" on a project card
Verify element "Workbench top bar" is visible

title: Switch active project from the TopBar dropdown
description: Verifies that the active project can be changed from the top bar after a project is opened.

steps.description: Click on "Open" on a project card
Click on the "Active project" dropdown in the workbench top bar
Click on a different project name in the project list
Verify the active project name in the top bar changes to the newly selected project

title: Select context 'default' and open the Model tab
description: Verifies selecting the 'default' context and switching to the Model tab loads the Model Editor for the active project.

steps.description: Click on "Open" on a project card
Click on the "Context" selector in the workbench top bar
Click on "Model" in the tab bar

title: Enable multi-context mode and select two contexts
description: Verifies multi-context mode can be enabled and two contexts can be selected for the opened project.

steps.description: Click on "Open" on a project card
Click on the TopBar control to enable multi-context mode
Click on the "Context" selector in the workbench top bar
Click on one context option in the list
Click on a second context option in the list

title: With two contexts selected, switch to SQL tab and verify editor readiness
description: Verifies switching to the SQL tab works with multi-context selection and the SQL editor is ready with context metadata visible.

steps.description: Click on "Open" on a project card
Click on the TopBar control to enable multi-context mode
Click on the "Context" selector in the workbench top bar
Click on one context option in the list
Click on a second context option in the list
Click on "SQL" in the tab bar

title: Attempt to select a non-existent context and verify error toast
description: Verifies that choosing an unavailable context shows a 'Context unavailable' toast and does not change the current context.

steps.description: Click on "Open" on a project card
Click on the "Context" selector in the workbench top bar
Type "non-existent-context" into the context selector search field
Verify the previously selected context value is still visible in the top bar

title: Switch between tabs (Model to SQL) and confirm each screen loads
description: Verifies tab switching works end-to-end by moving between Model and SQL views within an opened project.

steps.description: Click on "Open" on a project card
Click on "Model" in the tab bar
Click on "SQL" in the tab bar

title: Toggle theme control and verify visible UI change indicator
description: Verifies theme can be toggled from the workbench controls and a visible indicator reflects the change.

steps.description: Click on "Open" on a project card
Click on the "Theme" control in the workbench top bar
Click on the "Theme" control in the workbench top bar

title: Toggle user role control and verify role label changes
description: Verifies the user role control can be changed and the visible role indicator updates accordingly.

steps.description: Click on "Open" on a project card
Click on the "Role" control in the workbench top bar
Click on a different role option
Verify the role label in the top bar reflects the newly selected role

title: Open SQL file, edit, save, and view rendered SQL
description: Verifies a SQL file can be opened from the project tree, edited, saved successfully, and switched to rendered preview.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "SQL" tab in the TabBar
Click to open the file tree in the left Sidebar
Click the SQL file "001_main.sql" in the file tree
Type "\n-- e2e edit" into the SQL editor
Click the "Save" button in the SQL editor toolbar

title: Switch between prepared and rendered views after opening a SQL file
description: Verifies the user can toggle from prepared to rendered view and see the rendered SQL preview panel.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "SQL" tab in the TabBar
Click to open the file tree in the left Sidebar
Click the SQL file "001_main.sql" in the file tree
Click "Prepared" view toggle (if present) to ensure prepared mode is active
Verify element "Rendered SQL preview" is visible

title: Metadata panel shows Priority Chain for an opened SQL file
description: Verifies the right-side metadata panel can be opened and displays a Priority Chain section for the selected SQL file.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "SQL" tab in the TabBar
Click to open the file tree in the left Sidebar
Click the SQL file "001_main.sql" in the file tree
Click to open the right-side "Metadata" (or "Info") panel

title: Clicking an object in Priority Chain highlights reference in the editor
description: Verifies selecting an item from the metadata Priority Chain causes a visible highlight/selection in the SQL editor.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "SQL" tab in the TabBar
Click to open the file tree in the left Sidebar
Click the SQL file "001_main.sql" in the file tree
Click to open the right-side "Metadata" (or "Info") panel
Click the first clickable object listed under "Priority Chain"
Verify element "Highlighted SQL reference" is visible

title: Empty-content SQL file shows placeholder state
description: Verifies a SQL file with empty content renders an empty placeholder in the editor instead of failing silently.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "SQL" tab in the TabBar
Click to open the file tree in the left Sidebar
Click the SQL file that is known to load with empty content

title: Saving an empty-content SQL file shows error toast and retains unsaved changes
description: Verifies that a failed save shows an error message and the editor still contains the user’s unsaved edits.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "SQL" tab in the TabBar
Click to open the file tree in the left Sidebar
Click the SQL file that is known to load with empty content
Click the "Save" button in the SQL editor toolbar
Verify text "Failed to save file" is visible

title: After save failure, editor shows unsaved/error indicator
description: Verifies the UI indicates the file is still dirty or in an error state after a failed save attempt.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "SQL" tab in the TabBar
Click to open the file tree in the left Sidebar
Click the SQL file that is known to load with empty content
Type "-- change that should remain" into the SQL editor
Click the "Save" button in the SQL editor toolbar
Verify element "Unsaved changes indicator" is visible

title: Opening a different SQL file changes the editor content
description: Verifies switching files via the sidebar updates the editor to the newly selected file’s content.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "SQL" tab in the TabBar
Click to open the file tree in the left Sidebar
Click the SQL file "001_main.sql" in the file tree
Click a different SQL file in the file tree

title: Visual mode: edit target table, add field, import from catalog, and save successfully
description: Verifies a user can modify a model in Visual mode (target_table, add field, import catalog fields) and save with a success toast.

steps.description: Click on the project card or button that opens a project in the workspace (for example, "Open project").
Click on the "Model" tab in the TabBar.
Select model "Sales" from the model selector.
Fill "sales" into the target table name field.
Fill "customer_id" into the new field name input.
Verify element "Import from catalog" modal is visible.
Select an entity from the entity selector/list in the import modal.
Click on "Confirm" (or "Import") in the import modal.

title: Visual mode: cancel catalog import does not change the model
description: Verifies dismissing the catalog import modal does not apply imported fields and does not show a save success toast.

steps.description: Click on the project card or button that opens a project in the workspace (for example, "Open project").
Click on the "Model" tab in the TabBar.
Select model "Sales" from the model selector.
Verify element "Import from catalog" modal is visible.
Click on "Cancel" (or close icon) in the import modal.
Verify element "Import from catalog" modal is not visible.

title: Visual mode: save after adding a new field shows success
description: Verifies adding a single field in Visual mode and saving results in a visible success toast.

steps.description: Click on the project card or button that opens a project in the workspace (for example, "Open project").
Click on the "Model" tab in the TabBar.
Select model "Sales" from the model selector.
Fill "customer_id" into the new field name input.

title: YAML mode: edit YAML and save; visual representation updates accordingly
description: Verifies YAML edits can be saved and that the visual mode reflects the YAML changes after save.

steps.description: Click on the project card or button that opens a project in the workspace (for example, "Open project").
Click on the "Model" tab in the TabBar.
Select model "Sales" from the model selector.
Type "# e2e change" into the YAML editor.
Verify element "Visual" mode panel is visible.

title: YAML mode: malformed YAML blocks save and shows validation error
description: Verifies invalid YAML prevents saving and a visible validation error is shown.

steps.description: Click on the project card or button that opens a project in the workspace (for example, "Open project").
Click on the "Model" tab in the TabBar.
Select model "Sales" from the model selector.

title: YAML mode: after validation error, switching to Visual keeps prior state (no applied changes)
description: Verifies that when YAML save fails, switching to Visual mode does not reflect the invalid YAML changes.

steps.description: Click on the project card or button that opens a project in the workspace (for example, "Open project").
Click on the "Model" tab in the TabBar.
Select model "Sales" from the model selector.
Verify element "Visual" mode panel is visible.

title: Mode switching: Visual to YAML to Visual preserves the selected model context
description: Verifies switching between modes does not lose the currently selected model and the model editor remains open.

steps.description: Click on the project card or button that opens a project in the workspace (for example, "Open project").
Click on the "Model" tab in the TabBar.
Select model "Sales" from the model selector.

title: Save action availability: saving with no edits does not show an error
description: Verifies clicking Save without making edits does not present a visible error and remains on the model editor screen.

steps.description: Click on the project card or button that opens a project in the workspace (for example, "Open project").
Click on the "Model" tab in the TabBar.
Select model "Sales" from the model selector.
Verify element "Model" editor content is visible.

title: Open Lineage tab and view graph layout with summary counters for model Sales
description: Verifies that the Lineage tab loads a DAG graph and shows summary counters after selecting the Sales model.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "Lineage" tab in the TabBar
Click on model "Sales" in the model selector/list

title: Open node detail panel from lineage graph and open related SQL
description: Verifies that clicking a lineage node opens the detail panel and that 'Open related SQL' navigates to the SQL editor for the related file.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "Lineage" tab in the TabBar
Click on model "Sales" in the model selector/list
Click on node "stage_01" in the lineage graph
Click on "Open related SQL" in the detail panel

title: Use lineage search to find a node and open its detail panel
description: Verifies the lineage node search can locate a node by name and selecting the result opens the node detail panel.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "Lineage" tab in the TabBar

title: Navigate from lineage node detail to its target asset via Navigate to asset
description: Verifies that the 'Navigate to asset' action from the node detail panel opens the target asset in an editor/model screen.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "Lineage" tab in the TabBar
Click on "Navigate to asset" in the detail panel

title: Workflow cache missing state shows badge and rebuild error is surfaced
description: Verifies the UI displays the workflow cache missing badge and surfaces rebuild failure via status and toast message.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "Lineage" tab in the TabBar
Click on a model that shows a "workflow cache missing" state in the model selector/list
Verify text "workflow cache missing" is visible
Verify text "framework CLI failed" is visible

title: Detail panel shows expected metadata when selecting a lineage node
description: Verifies that selecting a node displays node metadata in the detail panel.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "Lineage" tab in the TabBar
Click on model "Sales" in the model selector/list
Click on node "stage_01" in the lineage graph

title: Lineage graph handles no visible node match from search
description: Verifies that searching for a non-existent node shows an appropriate empty/no-results state without opening the detail panel.

steps.description: Click on "Open project" in the Project Hub
Click on the "Lineage" tab in the TabBar
Verify element "Detail panel" is not visible

title: Run validation with filters and view grouped results
description: Verifies a user can run validation, apply severity/category filters, and see results grouped by severity.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click the "Validate" button in the top bar
Select a severity filter option (for example, "Error")
Select a rule category filter option (choose any available category)
Verify a validation progress indicator is visible
Verify validation results grouped by severity are visible

title: Inspect an issue and open QuickFix Preview modal
description: Verifies a user can open an issue from validation results and see the QuickFix Preview modal.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click the "Validate" button in the top bar
Verify validation results grouped by severity are visible
Click on an issue item from the results list (choose any visible error or warning)
Verify element "QuickFix Preview" modal is visible

title: Apply quick fix add_field and confirm editor and results update
description: Verifies applying the 'add_field' quick fix updates the editor content and the validation results reflect the change.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click the "Validate" button in the top bar
Click on an issue item that shows a suggested quick fix (choose one that lists "add_field")
Verify text "add_field" is visible in the QuickFix Preview modal
Click "Apply" in the QuickFix Preview modal
Verify text "Applied" or "Fix applied" is visible

title: Open Validate tab and view validation history list
description: Verifies a user can open the Validate tab and see validation history entries.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click the "Validate" tab in the tab bar
Verify text "History" or "Validation history" is visible
Verify a list of previous validation runs is visible
Scroll to the bottom of the history list
Verify at least one history entry remains visible after scrolling

title: Open a previous validation run from history and view run details with file links
description: Verifies a user can open a historical run and see run details including linked file locations.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click the "Validate" tab in the tab bar
Click a previous validation run entry from the history list
Verify text "Run details" or "Details" is visible
Verify an issue list for the selected run is visible
Verify a linked file location reference is visible (for example, a filename or a "Go to" link)

title: Run dqcr build with default context and view completion artifacts list
description: Verifies a user can run a dqcr build with the default context and see progress, logs, and a generated artifacts list on completion.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click on the "Build" tab in the tab bar
Verify an element "Build in progress" (or equivalent progress indicator) is visible
Verify text "Generated artifacts" (or equivalent artifacts section label) is visible

title: Verify streamed build logs appear during an active build run
description: Verifies that running a build shows streaming log output while the build is in progress.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click on the "Build" tab in the tab bar
Verify an element "Logs" (or a log panel/section header) is visible
Verify text "Build" (or similar log line prefix) is visible in the logs area

title: Switch engine to airflow and run a build
description: Verifies the user can select the airflow engine and start a build run, observing the in-progress state.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click on the "Build" tab in the tab bar
Verify an element "Build in progress" (or equivalent progress indicator) is visible
Verify an element "Build history" (or equivalent history section) is visible

title: Attempt to run build without selecting required options shows validation feedback
description: Verifies the UI prevents running a build when required selections (engine/context) are missing and displays visible validation messaging.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click on the "Build" tab in the tab bar
Verify text "Select engine" (or equivalent validation message) is visible
Verify an element "Build in progress" (or equivalent progress indicator) is not visible

title: Build failure shows failure toast and exposes error logs in bottom panel
description: Verifies that when a build run fails, the UI shows a visible failure notification and the logs panel displays error output.

steps.description: Click on an available project in the Project Hub to open it
Click on the "Build" tab in the tab bar
Click on the bottom panel "Logs" (or "Console") to open it
Verify text "Error" (or equivalent error output) is visible in the logs panel
